headline: Analyst Upgrades Put Bank of America (BAC) Back in Focus as Valuation Debate Widens
summary: Recent analyst upgrades of Bank of America (BAC) have put the stock back in focus, highlighting its earnings record, capital returns through dividends and buybacks, and valuation relative to its fundamentals. See our latest analysis for Bank of America. At a share price of US$49.38, Bank of America has a 7 day share price return of 4.55%, while the 90 day share price return shows a decline of 13.75%. headline: Bank of America downgrades US chemical stocks following price rally

headline: Carvana rating shifted to Neutral but BofA sees long-term potential
summary: Bank of America shifted its rating on Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA) to Neutral on Monday, signaling a more cautious near-term outlook for the online used-car retailer. The move reflects a mix of macroeconomic pressures and industry dynamics that have tempered earlier optimism, according to a note...

headline: CITGO Celebrates Resilience, Service and Community at the 130th Boston Marathon presented by Bank of America
summary: On Monday, April 20, 2026, runners from across the country will take on the 130th Boston Marathon presented by Bank of America, and CITGO Petroleum Corporation will be there to support the people and communities that power the spirit of Marathon Monday. headline: How Royal Caribbean’s New Tri‑Branded Credit Cards With Bank of America Could Shape RCL’s Investor Story
summary: Royal Caribbean Group and Bank of America have launched the Royal ONE and Royal ONE Plus Visa Signature tri-branded credit cards, allowing guests to earn and redeem rewards across Royal Caribbean, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea with tiered point multipliers, travel perks, and no foreign transaction fees. This tri-branded program deepens Royal Caribbean’s loyalty ecosystem by tying everyday card spending directly to cruise savings and onboard experiences across its three core brands. Next,...

headline: How The Banco Santander Chile (SNSE:BSANTANDER) Story Is Shifting With Fresh Analyst Debate
summary: The latest analyst update for Banco Santander Chile edges fair value from CLP 75.59 to CLP 76.30, a small shift that keeps the broader valuation range relatively steady. This change sits alongside a flurry of recent Street calls, where some firms have lifted targets while others, such as Goldman Sachs with its move from US$28 to US$29, still flag valuation concerns. As you read on, you will see how these differing price targets shape the evolving narrative around the stock and what that means...
